publicAppointment.smsTwilioFromToCombinationHelp: Twilio does not allow this sender number to reach that destination with your current setup. Common case: a US +1 long code cannot send to Bangladesh +880 until you fix routing in Twilio. Options: (1) Buy a Twilio number in Bangladesh (or the patient’s country) and set it as the SMS “From” number in Notification Settings, (2) Create a Messaging Service in Twilio and use a sender pool that supports that route, (3) In Twilio Console check Phone Numbers → your number → capabilities, Messaging → Geo permissions, and your account’s international SMS rules. The clinic may need to contact Twilio support to enable the From→To country pair.
publicAppointment.smsTwilioGeoPermissionHelp: Twilio is blocking SMS to that destination country. In Twilio Console open Messaging → Settings → Geo permissions (or Regulatory compliance) and enable the country shown on the “To” number. If patients use Bangladesh mobiles, set Default SMS country to BD under Notification Settings and ensure the patient record phone is +880… or a national number like 01… (not +91).
publicAppointment.smsUnavailableHelp: The clinic must enable SMS (Twilio) in admin Settings → Notification Settings. You can book as a new patient or call reception instead.
publicAppointment.successHint: You can close this page or return to the home page.
publicAppointment.verifyBeforeScheduleHint: Verify your phone first, then choose your visit time below.
